text:
Kasuga (カスガ, Kasuga) was a female servant of the Zoldyck Family who had the duty of taking care of Alluka Zoldyck after the previous servant Yasuha had wished to become a billionaire. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 28, Chapter 322 

Appearance

Kasuga was a young woman with long black hair, wearing the standard Zoldyck butler uniform. 

Personality

Little is known about Kasuga's personality. In spite of the rules of the Zoldyck household, she dated a man online and the two eventually became a couple. It was also said that she eventually started to love him more than her ill mother. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 28, Chapter 323 

Background

Kasuga grew up in an orphanage. She was then trained to become a butler for the Zoldyck family. Her mother was sick and the two could meet only once a month for thirty minutes. Shortly before her death, Kasuga had started to date a man she had met online. 

Plot

13 th Hunter Chairman Election arc

Illumi makes a deal with her that if she listens to Alluka's requests and completes them, he would overlook her forbidden romantic relationship with a man she's met on the internet. Much to her shock, she is asked for her liver, intestines, spine, and brain consecutively by Alluka. Kasuga fearfully declines each and every one of the 4 requests. As a result, the cost of the wish to become a billionaire made previously by Yasuha is thus transferred to her, her lover, and 65 other people, including her teachers and classmates inside the Zoldyck estate, all of whom are crushed to death at the same time. 

Abilities & Powers

As a butler of the Zoldyck Family, Kasuga was in constant training. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 5, Chapter 40 She could also open the Testing Gates, since according to Zebro, opening the gates is the bare minimum, Hunter × Hunter - Volume 5, Chapter 39 although the number of doors she could open is unknown.

Nen

Kasuga likely knew how to use Nen because she was a butler of the Zoldyck Family.

Manga Appearances

Trivia

In the Viz translation, when Alluka requests Kasuga's intestines, she calls her "Kusaga" —most likely a typo, as the request can only be made if Alluka knows the subject person's name.
